name: RP2040 Platform
description: This component contains platform-specific options for the RP2 platform, which covers both the RP2040 and RP2350 chips from the Raspberry Pi RP-series.

config_entries.label: Variant
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `board`, `variant`.

The RP2-series chip to target. One of `rp2040` or `rp2350`. This must match the hardware in use, or it will fail to flash.

config_entries.label: Enable Full Printf
config_entries.description: Enable full `FILE*`-based printf support. By default, ESPHome wraps `printf()`, `vprintf()`, and `fprintf()` with lightweight stubs that use `vsnprintf()` + `fwrite()`, saving ~9.2 KB of flash. ESPHome logging uses `snprintf`/`vsnprintf`, not libc printf, so these functions are typically unused unless an external component calls them. Set to `true` only if an external component needs full `FILE*`-based printf. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Watchdog Timeout
config_entries.description: The timeout to apply to the RP2040 watchdog. When the device hangs for that period of time, it will reboot. Defaults to `8388ms` (maximum). Set to `0s` to disable the watchdog entirely.
